description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a series of evenly spaced, parallel grooves running across its top surface, creating a striped pattern. The grooves are deep and regular, giving the block a distinctive ribbed appearance. The stone material appears dense and solid, with a slightly mottled, marbled texture visible in the lighter areas between the grooves. The sides of the block are smooth and unadorned, contrasting with the textured top. The coloration is primarily a dark gray to black, with the grooved surfaces reflecting a lighter, purplish-gray hue. This block is likely used as an obstacle or barrier in urban environments, such as on sidewalks, plazas, or as part of a traffic control installation.
description: A rectangular stone block with deep, parallel grooves on top, featuring a dark, marbled texture and a striped appearance. The sides are smooth and the top is ribbed, making it visually distinct.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full width of the block and the parallel grooves running horizontally across the top surface.
description_view_1: Side-angled view highlighting the depth and spacing of the grooves, with the stripes running vertically from this perspective.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the full width and the horizontal grooves on the top surface.
description_view_3: Opposite side-angled view, again emphasizing the vertical alignment of the grooves and their regular spacing.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or plaza as a barrier or obstacle
attribute_installation_method: embedded in concrete or set flush with pavement
attribute_weathering_pattern: No visible weathering; the surface appears clean and well-maintained with no signs of erosion or moss.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: isolated or aligned as part of a row of similar blocks
